sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After moving RIGHT, the character is at [23,37]x[35,49], and the green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49] with 126 pixels. The green frame healed from 99 to 126 pixels!
The red blocks are at [38,43]x[38,40] with 18 pixels and [38,40]x[44,46] with 9 pixels.
Looking at the image, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, and the green frame is to the right. The character's right edge is at column 49, which is the same as the green frame's right edge. So the character is overlapping with the green frame.
The green frame healed from 99 to 126 pixels. This is interesting - the green frame healed even though the character is still overlapping.
Let me think about this. The green frame's pixel count seems to depend on the character's position relative to the green frame:
- 144 when character doesn't overlap
- 135 when character overlaps slightly
- 126 when character overlaps more
- 99 when character overlaps deeply
- 126 when character overlaps even more (healed?)
This doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine.
Actually, looking at the segmentation, the green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49] with 126 pixels. The character is at [23,37]x[35,49]. The character's right edge is at column 49, and the green frame's right edge is also at column 49. So the character is overlapping with the green frame.
But the green frame healed from 99 to 126 pixels. This might be because the character moved to a different position relative to the green frame, causing less overlap.
Let me try rotating the character to see if the green frame heals further or if the level completes.
Actually, I notice the character is at [23,37]x[35,49], which means the character's bounding box extends from column 35 to 49. The green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49]. So the character overlaps with the green frame at columns [41, 49].
Looking at the image, the character is upright with feet at the bottom, and the green frame is to the right. The character's body is inside the green frame's interior.
Let me try rotating to see if the character fits better inside the green frame.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Character at [23,37]x[35,49] upright with feet at bottom, overlapping green frame at [29,49]x[41,49]. Green frame at 126 pixels (healed from 99). Character body inside green frame interior.
Plan: Try rotating to fit character inside green frame, or move UP/DOWN to align with frame opening.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try rotating to fit inside frame
action(['SPACE'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'G', 'R'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows [{min(rows)}, {max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}, {max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
